text: The surgeon navigates through the surgical field, identifying a detour and pressing on the area where nerves are visible. The tissue appears white and altered, possibly due to a previous surgery, with a round structure observed nearby.

The surgeon observes the obturator nerve and vein, noting that they are adjacent to a fatty tissue that is bleeding. The bladder ligament is visible in the middle of the surgical field, with open blood vessels nearby.
The surgeon requests a scissor and proceeds to manipulate tissue in the surgical field, indicating a specific area for the assistant to focus on.
